name: build-deployment-specialist
description: Specialized agent for managing builds, configurations, and deployment of Floating AI Copilot stack
scope: Build processes, environment configuration, CI/CD setup, local development environment

rules.description: .NET Core 10 uses dotnet CLI for build, restore, and run
Angular 19 uses ng CLI with npm package manager

commands.npm start: ng serve - Dev server at http://localhost:4200 with live reload

rules.loadingOrder: Base loaded first, then environment-specific overrides

rules.description: .NET local development uses auto-generated HTTPS certificates
rules.browsers: May need to bypass warning for self-signed cert on first run

rules.separation: Can be deployed independently or together

rules.addingDependency: Edit .csproj file directly or: dotnet add package {name}

rules.description: Frontend dependencies in package.json and package-lock.json
rules.lockfile: package-lock.json - Locks exact versions for reproducible installs

rules.description: Angular TypeScript settings with strict null checking
rules.strictMode: strict: true enables strict null checks

rules.description: Backend HTTPS port can be customized
Project does not have GitHub Actions or other CI/CD
rules.future: Consider adding .github/workflows/ for automated testing and deployment

steps: Terminal 1: cd api/src && dotnet run (Backend starts on localhost:7001)
Terminal 2: cd ui && npm start (Frontend starts on localhost:4200)
Browser: http://localhost:4200 (Angular dev server with live reload)
Make code changes in editor, browser auto-refreshes

productionBuild.frontend: npm run build (Creates dist/ folder with optimized bundle)
productionBuild.deployment: Deploy publish/ and dist/ to appropriate servers

backendWontStart.solution: Change port in Program.cs or end process using port 7001

futureEnhancements: Add GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow for automated testing
Extract API base URL to environment configuration
Add Docker support for containerized deployment
